zotero.abstractNote: Scale-equivariance is a desirable property for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in computer vision tasks where the scale of objects varies. While scale-equivariant CNNs have been proposed, they often rely on a fixed set of scales or a predefined scale-space. In this paper, we propose a method to learn the scale-space representation directly from data. We introduce a scale-equivariant convolution layer that learns the optimal scale-space sampling, allowing the network to adapt to the scale distribution of the input data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmarks, showing that our learned scale-equivariant CNNs outperform fixed-scale counterparts and achieve competitive results with state-of-the-art methods.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 19th International Joint Conference on Computer Vision, Imaging and Computer Graphics Theory and Applications
